title: When Interacting with Others: - Ask:
subtitle: Challenge Assumed Similarities
description:
When interacting with others:
- Ask: "What’s unique about this person’s perspective or experience?"
- Listen actively: Avoid projecting your own traits or beliefs onto them.
- Reflect: Notice when you assume similarities without evidence and correct yourself.

Example: Don’t assume a coworker has the same work style as you; ask about their preferences and needs.

lifeOsSource.checkIn: Check in when you actively avoid assuming someone is just like you.

faq.question: How do I track my progress with this habit?
faq.answer: Use the check-in method described above and track it in the Life OS app for consistent progress monitoring.

faq.question: What if I miss a day or forget to do this habit?
faq.answer: Don't worry! Life OS habits are designed to be flexible. Just get back on track the next day without judgment.

faq.question: How long does it take to form this habit?
faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Identify your current communication habits by tracking how often you ask clarifying questions and the time spent listening in interactions for a few days.

howTo.title: Practice the unique perspective question
howTo.summary: In your next interaction, ask, 'What’s unique about your perspective or experience here?' Listen actively without interrupting and note their response.

howTo.title: Paraphrase and set next steps
howTo.summary: After the person responds, paraphrase their main point to ensure understanding and agree on a concrete next step to take.

howTo.title: Reflect on outcomes
howTo.summary: After your interactions, evaluate how asking the question influenced decisions and communication clarity. Note any changes in follow-up needs.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and insights in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and refine your approach. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/assumed-similarity-bias-check/.
